about.bio.1: My curiosity spans the full depth of complex systems — from how industrial operations fail at the human interface, to how financial platforms lose users inside a single workflow step. That drive to find the friction others missed is what has shaped twelve years of designing enterprise platforms, operational software, and workflow-driven products that expert users depend on daily.
about.bio.2: Ultimately, what colleagues tell me sets me apart is the ability to hold systems thinking and visual craft simultaneously — and to drive ambiguous, high-stakes design problems from problem framing all the way through to a shipped product that measurably moves the needle.
about.quote.text: Great operational design starts with deep respect for the expert user and ends with a workflow that feels inevitable.
about.title: A designer built for complex systems
about.value.0.desc: Every platform problem starts with sitting beside the operator who lives inside it — watching where the system fails them before a single frame is designed.
about.value.0.title: Operator-first
about.value.1.desc: The interaction models that underpin a platform are only as strong as the research that challenged every assumption behind them.
about.value.1.title: Research-validated
about.value.2.desc: Shipping a system that engineering trusts requires speaking their language — constraints, states, edge cases, and all.
about.value.2.title: Engineering-fluent
about.value.3.desc: Systems thinking and visual craft are not opposing forces. The best operational platforms prove both in the same interface.
about.value.3.title: Systems thinker

modal.project.0.challenge: Within four months of deployment, the AI-assisted behavioral insights platform directly informed targeted operator engagement strategies and reversed a deteriorating business trajectory. The organization moved from a –17% operating margin to +2%, improved operator retention and subscription renewals, increased average transaction size, and reduced onboarding friction for new platform participants — demonstrating how thoughtfully applied generative AI, grounded in deep operator research and disciplined design, can drive measurable platform-level outcomes.

modal.project.0.outcome.0: Reversed negative platform trajectory — operating margin moved from –17% to +2% within four months of deployment.
modal.project.0.outcome.1: 15% improvement in operator retention and subscription renewals driven by real, actionable behavioral insights surfaced through research synthesis.
modal.project.0.outcome.2: 10% increase in average transaction value and operator lifetime value through data-informed engagement strategy.
modal.project.0.outcome.3: Reduced platform onboarding friction by proactively surfacing operator needs and addressing them before they became drop-off points.
modal.project.0.outcome.4: Scaled organizational intelligence across ~40 users in sales, operations, field development, and executive leadership — embedding research insights into daily platform decisions.
modal.project.0.outcomes.heading: Key Outcomes
modal.project.0.overview: An AI-assisted operator insights platform built on live business intelligence data — combining behavioral, demographic, and workflow signals to help teams understand not just who their operators were, but how and why they made decisions within the platform.

Within four months, it enabled more precise targeting and smarter interactions that helped move our operating margin from –17% to +2%, while strengthening operator trust and long-term engagement.

modal.project.0.title: AI Operator Insights Platform
modal.project.1.challenge: I architected and led delivery of a comprehensive platform design system to modernize a fragmented operational software experience spanning marketing, sales, operator-facing tools, and internal communication platforms. The goal was not only visual renewal but a durable system improving consistency, scalability, and engineering delivery speed — aligning every design decision with platform outcomes and operator needs.
modal.project.1.kpi.0.label: Reduction in Delivery Time
modal.project.1.kpi.1.label: Designers Impacted
modal.project.1.kpi.2.label: More Design Deliverables
modal.project.1.kpi.3.label: Designer Hours Saved
modal.project.1.outcome.0: Established consistent interaction patterns and component standards across all platform surfaces — eliminating inconsistency between designers and engineering teams.
modal.project.1.outcome.1: Created a shared source of truth for component specifications, interaction models, and design standards — clarifying scope and deliverables for all platform artifacts.
modal.project.1.outcome.2: Aligned platform UX strategy with business goals by embedding design intent directly into system standards and engineering documentation.
modal.project.1.outcome.3: Increased confidence and velocity of design team through clear, repeatable patterns — enabling faster iteration on complex operational workflows.
modal.project.1.outcome.4: Earned executive trust by providing visible, consistent standards referenced across all platform deliverables org-wide.
modal.project.1.outcomes.heading: Key Outcomes
modal.project.1.overview: Delivered as a hybrid greenfield and progressive migration, the system lives in Figma and is referenced directly in the CMS — enabling designers, engineers, and platform leaders to operate from a shared source of truth. Adoption materially improved delivery speed, interaction quality, and team confidence, transforming design from a reactive service into a scalable, strategic platform capability.

modal.project.1.quote.text: This system changed how we work. Delivery time was cut by roughly 50%. Visual and interaction quality jumped by about 80%. We went from weeks assembling concepts to delivering clean, standardized prototypes in days. It raised the bar for every platform project.
modal.project.1.skills.heading: Skills Applied
modal.project.1.title: Design System Architect
modal.project.2.challenge: I defined the experience strategy and end-to-end interaction design for a new multi-step operator onboarding and advancement workflow. The core challenge is twofold: making it genuinely easy for new and existing operators to complete complex multi-step enrollment flows within compliance requirements, and ensuring participants clearly understand the value of the platform — without confusion, friction, or ambiguity at any critical workflow decision point.
modal.project.2.kpi.0.label: Target NPS Increase
modal.project.2.kpi.1.label: Workflow Touchpoints Redesigned
modal.project.2.kpi.2.label: Projected Workflow Drop-Off ↓
modal.project.2.kpi.3.label: Target Completion
modal.project.2.outcome.0: Clarified platform value proposition at each workflow decision point — reducing operator hesitation and confusion during multi-step enrollment.
modal.project.2.outcome.1: Improved operator trust and transparency through clearer workflow state communication, progress indicators, and guided success states.
modal.project.2.outcome.2: Enabled subscription-led platform activation as a first-class workflow step rather than an optional afterthought.
modal.project.2.outcome.3: Reduced friction for operators advancing through complex multi-step platform participation workflows.
modal.project.2.outcome.4: Established a scalable workflow architecture aligned with long-term operator retention and platform growth objectives.
modal.project.2.outcomes.heading: Key Outcomes
modal.project.2.overview: This initiative reframes operator onboarding as a guided progression through a well-defined workflow — rather than a transactional gate. Accomplished by aligning interaction design, platform mechanics, and multi-step flow architecture across landing, enrollment, and activation surfaces. The result is a scalable workflow foundation that improves completion rates, reduces drop-off at critical decision points, and supports sustainable operator growth across the platform.

modal.project.2.quote.text: By simplifying the operator workflow and aligning it with subscription-based platform engagement, we plan to reduce friction at the most fragile points of the multi-step journey. Early analysis shows faster completion times, higher subscription attachment at enrollment, and improved operator confidence.

modal.project.2.title: Operator Onboarding Workflow
modal.project.4.challenge: Leading the end-to-end redesign of a critical multi-step operational workflow for a high-volume platform experiencing severe drop-off — exceeding 80% at key decision points — driven by operator trust failures and workflow patterns that diverged significantly from modern platform standards. This was a platform recovery effort, not a visual refresh — requiring deep operator research, workflow architecture rethinking, and close engineering collaboration.

modal.project.4.outcome.0: Clear, confidence-driven workflow experience addressing operator uncertainty at the most critical multi-step decision points.
modal.project.4.outcome.1: Reduced operator cognitive load by eliminating unnecessary steps, ambiguous states, and workflow dead ends.
modal.project.4.outcome.2: Enabled subscription enrollment as a first-class workflow action embedded directly in the operational flow.
modal.project.4.outcome.3: Achieved parity with modern platform UX standards — closing the gap with best-in-class operational workflow design.
modal.project.4.outcome.4: Measurable improvement in platform retention and trust metrics through isolated, research-validated workflow improvements.
modal.project.4.outcomes.heading: Key Outcomes
modal.project.4.overview: Restoring operator trust, reducing workflow cognitive load, and enabling revenue-positive behaviors directly within the core operational flow — partnering with executive leadership, PMO, and engineering to establish a research-validated, design-led roadmap that aligns every interaction decision with measurable platform outcomes.

modal.project.4.quote.text: Reframing this operational workflow as a platform growth system rather than a technical necessity — early validations expect meaningful reductions in drop-off, faster workflow completion, and measurable lift in operator subscription adoption — directly impacting platform revenue and operator trust.

modal.project.4.title: Operational Workflow Redesign
modal.project.5.challenge: Leading the experience strategy and interaction design for a new platform-wide intelligent search system to address a critical trust and relevance failure. Legacy search returned inaccurate results — forcing operators to rely on external tools to find what they needed within the platform — directly undermining workflow efficiency and platform confidence.
modal.project.5.kpi.0.label: AI-Powered Search Results
modal.project.5.kpi.1.label: Result Accuracy in SERP
modal.project.5.kpi.2.label: Platform Revenue & Growth
modal.project.5.kpi.3.label: Official Kickoff Date
modal.project.5.outcome.0: Restore operator trust in platform search by prioritizing intent-matched, exact results in the most visible workflow positions.
modal.project.5.outcome.1: Reduce operator effort by intelligently guiding searches that yield ambiguous or null results — rather than failing silently.
modal.project.5.outcome.2: Accelerate operator paths through platform workflows via intelligent content recommendations and contextual surfacing logic.
modal.project.5.outcome.3: Unify discovery across products, content, support, and platform resources into a single, coherent operational experience.
modal.project.5.outcome.4: Establish intelligent search as a strategic platform tool and retention lever supporting operator engagement and long-term platform growth.
modal.project.5.outcomes.heading: Key Outcomes
modal.project.5.overview: Platform search is a core operational workflow layer — not a utility. By combining deterministic relevance logic with a custom AI API layer, the new system is designed to deliver exact, high-confidence results, guide operators when intent is unclear, and surface contextual recommendations that accelerate workflow completion and platform engagement.

modal.project.5.quote.text: By rebuilding platform search around operator intent and workflow relevance rather than keyword matching alone, we reduce friction at the earliest stage of the operator journey.
